subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: multilateral + independent (IMF, World Bank, MCC, WHO/CDC, V-Dem, Freedom House, RSF, TI, HRW) + international press.

A post-civil-war democracy with two consecutive peaceful turnovers (Boakai defeated Weah, took office Jan 2024) and an in-progress War and Economic Crimes Court (an office and draft bills by 2025, no functioning tribunal yet).

Standout features: a heavily dollarized, iron-ore-led growth economy (~5% on ArcelorMittal) with contained inflation, against an Ebola-scarred health system, near-absent electrification/roads, deep rural poverty, and persistent corruption. Negative with a modest near-term positive tilt.

economic.notes: An IMF-programmed, dollarized economy with contained inflation but a narrow commodity base.

fiscal_state.notes: On an IMF ECF (approved Sept 2024; 2nd review Oct 2025) with improving discipline, but a very low revenue base and high aid dependence. (IMF PR 25/325)

monetary_independence.notes: Heavy dollarization severely weakens monetary transmission; the CBL's policy traction is structurally limited. (IMF; Coface)

reserve_currency_intl_monetary.notes: The Liberian dollar is not a reserve currency; the economy runs largely on the US dollar as a de-facto medium of exchange (imported stability, not LRD strength). (IMF)

inflation.notes: Relatively contained and falling, anchored by dollarization - a comparative bright spot. (IMF; World Bank)

banking_stability.notes: Small and dollarized with limited depth and legacy asset-quality weaknesses. (IMF)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: A narrow commodity base (iron ore via ArcelorMittal ~89% of output, rubber, gold), price-cycle exposed. (IMF; Coface)

institutional.notes: Functioning elections but persistent corruption, impunity, and thin administration.

rule_of_law.notes: Persistent corruption and impunity (CPI ~27/100) despite functioning electoral institutions. (TI; Freedom House)

statistical_integrity.notes: Weak capacity, IMF-engaged on macro data. (IMF)

civil_service_capacity.notes: Thin and under-resourced, with minimal reach into rural counties. (World Bank)

press_freedom.notes: Generally pluralistic media, with continued pressure on journalists. (RSF; Freedom House)

political_social.notes: Two decades of post-war peace and repeated peaceful turnovers, on a fragile low-capacity base.

political_stability.notes: Two decades of post-war peace and a second consecutive peaceful handover (Boakai took office Jan 2024) - stable but on a fragile foundation. (Freedom House; IMF)

civil_liberties.notes: 'Partly Free' (FH ~64), with real political rights tempered by impunity and violence against women. (Freedom House)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: No major refugee or migration-treatment crisis; limited data. (inference)

social_cohesion.notes: A still-raw civil-war legacy - the unresolved war-crimes accountability question both threatens and could heal cohesion. (HRW; JusticeInfo)

demographics.notes: Young and fast-growing; dividend potential offset by very low human capital and ~81% rural poverty. (World Bank)

geopolitical.notes: Historically close US ties, conflict-free since 2003, with peace holding after UNMIL's departure.

alliance_reliability.notes: Historically close US ties and Western/multilateral alignment; non-belligerent and donor-supported. (Coface; inference)

conflict_involvement.notes: No armed conflict - the second civil war ended in 2003 and peace has held. (Freedom House; ACLED)

sanctions_capital_controls.notes: Not broadly sanctioned (some targeted corruption designations); an open, dollarized economy with few controls. (US Treasury OFAC; IMF)

physical_practical.notes: Among the world's weakest health and infrastructure, Ebola-scarred, with coastal flood risk.

climate.notes: ND-GAIN bottom-30 - intense-rainfall flooding, coastal erosion on the Monrovia shore, post-war low readiness. (ND-GAIN; WB)

healthcare.notes: Among the world's weakest (~0.84 physicians/1,000), still recovering from the 2014-15 Ebola epidemic that killed >8% of health workers. (WHO; CDC)

infrastructure.notes: One of the world's lowest electrification rates and only ~20% of roads paved. (MCC; World Bank)

crime_safety.notes: Elevated urban crime (notably Monrovia) and weak policing, but no organised insurgency. (OSAC)

summary: A post-civil-war democracy that has delivered two consecutive peaceful electoral turnovers - most recently Joseph Boakai's defeat of George Weah in 2023, taking office in 2024 - and is pursuing transitional justice via a War and Economic Crimes Court that, as of 2025, exists as an office and draft bills but not yet a functioning tribunal. The heavily dollarized economy is growing ~5% on an ArcelorMittal-led iron-ore expansion with contained inflation and an on-track IMF ECF, but rests on a narrow commodity base and the US dollar substitutes for genuine monetary sovereignty. The binding constraints are physical and institutional: an Ebola-scarred health system among the world's weakest, near-absent electrification and roads, deep rural poverty, and persistent corruption. Negative overall, with a modest near-term positive tilt.
